name: ABS
description: Strong, heat- and impact-resistant — and priced about the same as PLA and PETG, so an easy step up when you need durability. Warps badly; needs an enclosure and ventilation.

name: ABS
description: Strong, heat- and impact-resistant — and priced about the same as PLA and PETG, so an easy step up when you need durability. Warps badly; needs an enclosure and ventilation. Fiber-reinforced (stiffer/stronger); abrasive — use a hardened nozzle.

name: ASA
description: ABS's UV-stable cousin: the top choice for parts that live outdoors in the sun. Warps; needs an enclosure.

name: ASA
description: ABS's UV-stable cousin: the top choice for parts that live outdoors in the sun. Warps; needs an enclosure. Lightweight foaming grade.

name: ASA
description: ABS's UV-stable cousin: the top choice for parts that live outdoors in the sun. Warps; needs an enclosure. Fiber-reinforced (stiffer/stronger); abrasive — use a hardened nozzle.

name: PA6
description: Tough, wear-resistant nylon with some flex — great for gears and living hinges. Soaks up moisture fast; dry well and enclose.

name: PA6
description: Tough, wear-resistant nylon with some flex — great for gears and living hinges. Soaks up moisture fast; dry well and enclose.

name: PAHT
description: High-temperature carbon-fiber nylon for demanding engineering parts. Stiff and strong; abrasive, needs drying and an enclosure.

name: PC
description: Extremely strong and heat-resistant engineering plastic. Demanding — high temperatures, an enclosure, and thorough drying.

name: PC
description: Extremely strong and heat-resistant engineering plastic. Demanding — high temperatures, an enclosure, and thorough drying. Flame-retardant grade.

name: PET
description: Stiff, strong, heat- and weather-resistant carbon-fiber engineering filament. Abrasive — hardened nozzle, drying, enclosure.

name: PETG
description: Tough, weather- and chemical-resistant all-rounder for functional and outdoor parts. Easy to print, but dry it first.

name: PETG
description: Tough, weather- and chemical-resistant all-rounder for functional and outdoor parts. Easy to print, but dry it first. Fiber-reinforced (stiffer/stronger); abrasive — use a hardened nozzle.

name: PETG
description: Tough, weather- and chemical-resistant all-rounder for functional and outdoor parts. Easy to print, but dry it first. High-flow — faster printing.

name: PETG
description: Tough, weather- and chemical-resistant all-rounder for functional and outdoor parts. Easy to print, but dry it first.

name: PLA
description: Easiest and cheapest to print — ideal for models, prototypes, and indoor parts. Brittle and softens in heat or sun; drying optional. Lightweight foaming grade.

name: PVA
description: Water-soluble support that dissolves for clean supports under complex prints. Extremely moisture-sensitive.

name: TPU
description: Rubber-like and flexible — gaskets, grips, and phone cases. Bends and absorbs impact instead of snapping; prints slowly, keep dry. Softest (85A) — very stretchy.
